name: smith machine incline tricep extension
instructions: Adjust the seat of the smith machine so that the bar is at shoulder height.
Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms fully, lifting the bar off the rack and holding it directly above your chest.
Lower the bar slowly towards your forehead, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the bar back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Adjust the seat of the smith machine so that the bar is at shoulder height. Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the ground. Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Extend your arms fully, lifting the bar off the rack and holding it directly above your chest. conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
